## Third-Party Licenses

RagEx uses several open-source libraries. Here are the key dependencies and their licenses:

### Core Dependencies

| Library | Version | License | Purpose |
|---------|---------|---------|---------|
| [Elixir](https://elixir-lang.org/) | 1.16+ | Apache 2.0 | Programming language |
| [Ecto](https://hexdocs.pm/ecto/Ecto.html) | 3.11+ | Apache 2.0 | Database wrapper |
| [Ecto SQLite3](https://hexdocs.pm/ecto_sqlite3/Ecto.Adapters.SQLite3.html) | 0.18+ | MIT | SQLite adapter |
| [Nx](https://github.com/elixir-nx/nx) | 0.6+ | Apache 2.0 | Numerical computing |
| [Plug.Cowboy](https://hexdocs.pm/plug_cowboy/Plug.Cowboy.html) | 2.7+ | Apache 2.0 | HTTP server |
| [Jason](https://github.com/michalmuskala/jason) | 1.4+ | Apache 2.0 | JSON encoding/decoding |
| [FileSystem](https://github.com/falood/file_system) | 0.2+ | MIT | File system monitoring |

### Development Dependencies

| Library | Version | License | Purpose |
|---------|---------|---------|---------|
| [ExUnit](https://hexdocs.pm/ex_unit/ExUnit.html) | Built-in | Apache 2.0 | Testing framework |
| [ExDoc](https://github.com/elixir-lang/ex_doc) | 0.30+ | Apache 2.0 | Documentation generation |
| [Credo](https://github.com/rrrene/credo) | 1.7+ | MIT | Code analysis |
| [Dialyxir](https://github.com/jeremyjh/dialyxir) | 1.3+ | Apache 2.0 | Static analysis |

## License Compatibility

### Compatible Licenses

The MIT License is compatible with most open-source licenses, including:

- **Apache 2.0**: Full compatibility
- **BSD 3-Clause**: Full compatibility
- **GPL 2.0/3.0**: Compatible for most use cases
- **LGPL**: Compatible for most use cases
- **MIT**: Full compatibility

### Commercial Use

RagEx can be used in commercial projects under the MIT License. This includes:

- **Proprietary software**: Include RagEx in closed-source applications
- **SaaS platforms**: Use RagEx in hosted services
- **Enterprise software**: Integrate RagEx into enterprise solutions
- **Consulting**: Use RagEx in client projects

### Redistribution Requirements

When redistributing RagEx (modified or unmodified):

1. **Include the license**: Copy the MIT License text
2. **Include copyright**: Preserve the copyright notice
3. **Include source**: Provide source code if requested
4. **Attribution**: Credit RagEx in your documentation

## Trademark Policy

### RagEx Trademark

"RagEx" is a trademark of the RagEx Team. The trademark covers:

- The name "RagEx"
- The RagEx logo and branding
- Associated visual elements

### Trademark Usage

**Permitted uses:**
- Reference RagEx in documentation
- Mention RagEx in blog posts and articles
- Use RagEx in educational materials
- Include RagEx in software acknowledgments

**Restricted uses:**
- Use "RagEx" as your product name
- Create derivative works using the RagEx name
- Use RagEx branding for competing products
- Imply endorsement or partnership

### Logo Usage

The RagEx logo can be used when:

- Crediting RagEx in your project
- Writing about RagEx
- Including RagEx in presentations
- Linking to RagEx documentation

**Guidelines:**
- Don't modify the logo
- Don't use the logo for competing products
- Maintain appropriate spacing around the logo
- Use the logo in context with RagEx

## Patent Policy

### Patent Grant

The MIT License includes an implicit patent grant. Contributors grant:

- **Patent rights**: Rights to any patents covering their contributions
- **Defensive termination**: Rights terminate if you sue for patent infringement
- **Scope**: Covers patents necessary to use the software

### Patent Considerations

- **Contributors**: Ensure you have rights to grant patent licenses
- **Users**: Be aware of potential patent issues in your domain
- **Defensive**: Consider defensive patent strategies

## Legal Disclaimer

### No Warranty

RagEx is provided "as is" without warranty of any kind. This includes:

- **Functionality**: No guarantee the software works as expected
- **Security**: No guarantee against security vulnerabilities
- **Compatibility**: No guarantee of compatibility with other software
- **Performance**: No guarantee of specific performance characteristics

### Limitation of Liability

The RagEx Team is not liable for:

- **Direct damages**: Loss of data, revenue, or business opportunities
- **Indirect damages**: Consequential or incidental damages
- **Special damages**: Punitive or exemplary damages
- **Third-party claims**: Claims from third parties

### Risk Assessment

Before using RagEx in production:

1. **Evaluate risks**: Assess potential risks in your environment
2. **Test thoroughly**: Conduct comprehensive testing
3. **Monitor usage**: Implement monitoring and alerting
4. **Have backups**: Maintain data backups and recovery procedures
5. **Consider alternatives**: Evaluate alternative solutions

## Compliance

### Open Source Compliance

When using RagEx in commercial projects:

1. **License compliance**: Ensure MIT License requirements are met
2. **Attribution**: Properly attribute RagEx and dependencies
3. **Source availability**: Make source code available if required
4. **Documentation**: Document your use of RagEx

### Export Compliance

RagEx may be subject to export control laws. Consider:

- **Encryption**: RagEx may contain cryptographic functionality
- **Export restrictions**: Check applicable export control regulations
- **Compliance**: Ensure compliance with local laws
- **Documentation**: Maintain appropriate documentation

## Contact

For legal questions or concerns:

- **Email**: legal@ragex.dev
- **GitHub**: Create an issue with "legal" label
- **Address**: [Legal contact information]

## Changes to License

### License Updates

The RagEx Team reserves the right to:

- **Update license**: Change license terms for future versions
- **Clarify terms**: Provide clarifications to existing terms
- **Add conditions**: Add reasonable conditions to usage
- **Notify changes**: Provide notice of significant changes

### Grandfathering

Existing users may be grandfathered under previous license terms for:

- **Current versions**: Versions already in use
- **Reasonable transition**: Time to adapt to new terms
- **Compatibility**: Maintain compatibility where possible
